channel.title: Wedding Cookbook on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Wedding Cookbook on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Zesty Asian Chicken Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Isabelle Dorval

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup rice vinegar
  • 8 tsp reduced sodium soy sauce
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp cilantro, chopped
  • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1/2 tsp sesame oil
  • 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
  • 4-3 oz boned and skinned chicken breast halves
  • 2 cups broccoli florets
  • 2 carrots, cut into 1 1/2 inch long matchsticks
  • 1 cup (6 ozs.) pitted prunes, snipped into halves
  • 3 green onions, sliced
  • 4 lettuce leaves

Instructions

To make dressing/marinade, in medium bowl whisk vinegar, soy, cilantro, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and pepper flakes. Remove 1/4 cup of mixture into a shallow 8 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts in baking dish. Turn to coat both sides. Let chicken marinate for 30 minutes.

item.title: Tomato Soup Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Emily and Robert Desbois

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 lemon Jello

  • 1 can tomato soup

  • 1 small cream cheese

  • 3 sticks celery, chopped

  • 1 medium onion, chopped

  • ¾ cup almonds, chopped

  • 1 cup Miracle Whip

Instructions

Heat Jello, soup and cream cheese until melted. Add other ingredients. Put green peppers on top if desired.

Place in fridge. Make a day ahead.

item.title: Spinach Salad with Strawberries
item.description:
Submitted by Patti and Darren Henri

Cousins of the Bride

  • Spinach, washed and torn
  • 1 pint of fresh strawberries, washed and sliced
  • ½ cup slivered almonds

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ to ½ cup of sugar
  • 2 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 1 tbsp poppy seeds
  • ¼ tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 ½ tsp chopped onion
  • ¼ tsp paprika
  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • ¼ cup cider vinegar

Instructions

Make the dressing the day before. Pour over salad, toss and serve.

item.title: Salade d'Amour
item.description:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau

Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked rice, cooled
  • 1 ½ cup fresh, chopped spinach
  • 3 stalks celery, cut diagonally
  • ¼ cup raisins
  • ½ cup green peppers, chopped
  • ½ cup red peppers, chopped
  • 3 sprigs fresh parsley
  • ½ cup green onions, chopped
  • 1 cup fresh, sliced mushrooms
  • 2 cups fresh (not canned) bean sprouts
  • ½ cup cashews

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 2 garlic buds (or more if you like)

Instructions

Put dressing in a jar and add raisins. Let stand overnight in refrigerator. Mix all ingredients together and pour dressing. Let sit in refrigerator for one hour before serving.

item.title: Rice Greek Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Lynn Sayer

Cousin of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked and cooled, Uncle Ben’s Converted Brand rice
  • 3/4 cup English cucumber, cut into slices
  • 3/4 cup tomatoes, diced
  • 3/4 cup green and red peppers, diced
  • 1/3 cup red onion, diced
  • 1 cup Feta cheese, cut into large dice
  • 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup olive oil
  • 1/4 cup red wine vinegar
  • 1 garlic, minced (about 1 tsp)
  • 1 tsp rubbed oregano
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp pepper

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine cooked rice with all diced vegetables, diced cheese and olives.

item.title: Party Lime Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Deanna and Larry Shank

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can crushed pineapple
  • 1 small package of lime Jello
  • 1 cup whipping cream
  • 1 cup chopped celery
  • 6 ozs cream cheese, room temperature
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Instructions

Heat pineapple. Dissolve lime Jello in pineapple. Stir well. Set in refrigerator until partially set.

Whip cream. Beat in cream cheese. Add nuts and celery. Add cream mixture to pineapple mixture.

item.title: Pasta Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup oil
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 2 ½ tbsp sugar
  • ¼ cup concentrated orange juice
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 1 tsp crushed red pepper
  • ½ tsp basil
  • black pepper
  • salt

Instructions

Cook choice of pasta, drain and add the dressing. Add crushed feta cheese, parmesan cheese, grapes (red and green), red and green peppers, olives and celery.

Chill and serve.

item.title: Lovely Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Ellie and Mike Bell

Cousins of the Groom

  • 1 green pepper
  • 1 red pepper
  • 2 green onions
  • 2 celery stalks
  • ¼ lb mushrooms
  • ¼ lb spinach (1 bag)
  • 1 cup cooked rice
  • 4 ozs cashew nuts, optional
  • 1 tbsp fresh parsley

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • ½ cup cooking oil
  • ¼ cup soya sauce
  • 1 garlic clove, minced

Instructions

Marinate dressing for one hour before using. Add to above salad 5-10 minutes before serving.

item.title: Good Coleslaw
item.description:
Submitted by Bev Bell and Lorne Mick

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

  • 1 medium cabbage
  • 1 medium onion
  • 2 grated carrots
  • 1/2 green pepper, optional
  • 1/2 red pepper, optional
  • 1 stick of celery, optional

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup oil
  • 1 cup white vinegar
  • 2/3 cup sugar
  • 2 tsp salt
  • celery salt, a little honey and chopped garlic are optional

Instructions

Chop, shred, grate, hack the veggies into a bowl. Bring oil, vinegar, sugar and salt to a boil, stirring to dissolve. Cool a little and pour over veggies. Tip the bowl to allow dressing to run over cabbage, etc.

item.title: Easy Taco Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Sandra and Steve Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb ground beef, browned
  • 1 large bag of Doritos, crushed into small pieces
  • 2 cups grated cheddar cheese
  • 1 medium onion, diced
  • 1 green pepper, diced
  • 1 tomato, diced
  • 1 small head of lettuce, chopped
  • 1 bottle of Kraft Catalina dressing

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. If done ahead, do not add the Doritos or dressing until served.

item.title: Broccoli Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Tracey and Pierre Beaudry

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

  • 1/2 cup raisins
  • 1/2 cup shelled sunflower seeds
  • 1 medium red onion, chopped
  • 1/2 lb crisp, crumbled bacon
  • 3 heads broccoli florets

Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup Miracle Whip
  • 2 tsp rice vinegar
  • 1/3 cup white sugar

Instructions

Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.

item.title: Best Damn Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Julie and Dave Pigeau

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 head of lettuce, chopped
  • 3 ribs of celery, chopped
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 2 cups partially thawed peas
  • 2 cups Hellman’s (must be Hellman’s mayonnaise)
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • bacon bits

Instructions

In a rectangle pan, layer all ingredients beginning with lettuce, celery, onions, and peas. Spread mayonnaise over top. Sprinkle with sugar, then parmesan cheese. Put in the fridge for 24 hours. Sprinkle with bacon bits just before serving.

item.title: "Cook Up" Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Naz and Brian Lloyd

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 ½ lbs of skinless chicken (cut up 2 whole chickens)
  • 1 ½ cup rice
  • 1-540 mL can black-eyed peas
  • 1-400 mL can coconut milk
  • 3 tbsp butter
  • 3 tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp basil
  • 3 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 1 large onion, minced
  • pinch of black pepper
  • salt to taste

Instructions

Wash chicken and cut into pieces (leave bones in). Place chicken in a large bowl. Blend in onion, garlic, soya sauce, basil, pepper and salt.

item.title: Sausage and Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Lori Girard

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can of tomatoes, or stewed tomatoes
  • one onion, cut in long slivers
  • 1 pkg beef bouillon
  • ¾ cup water
  • basil, garlic, salt and pepper

Instructions

Fry onion. Add other ingredients. Thicken sauce with corn starch. Fry breakfast sausage. Slice sausage and add to sauce.

Pour sauce with sausage over white rice.

item.title: Baked Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Linda Desrochers

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 cups of Uncle Ben’s converted rice
  • 4 cups water
  • ½ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 pkg onion soup mix
  • 1 can drained mushrooms
  • 1 can drained peas

Instructions

Mix all ingredients in roast pan. Place in oven for one hour at 350 degrees.

item.title: Poor Man's Pasta
item.description:
Submitted by Derek Forgie

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • Macaroni
  • Cheese Dust

Instructions

Fill a metal container with a water-like substance. Find a way to make it hot enough for the water to make a boiling noise. Place several noodles into the noisy water.

Stir the noodles with a pointy or rounded stick until they become limpanized. Drain the leftover water into a plant that looks cold and thirsty.

item.title: Penné with Peppers, Mushrooms, and Sausage
item.description:
Submitted by Marleen and Bill Tregenza

Godparents of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 red bell peppers
  • 14 ozs fresh mushrooms
  • 8 ozs sausage, remove from casing
  • 2 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp oregano
  • 1 tbsp thyme
  • 2 garlic cloves
  • ½ cup fresh parsley
  • grated parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Cut peppers into ½ inch diagonal pieces. Place in a 9x13 inch baking dish. Drizzle with 1 tbsp of olive oil, salt and pepper to taste. Bake until charred (45 minutes), stir occasionally. Remove.

item.title: Penné with Italian Sausage, Tomato, and Herbs
item.description:
Submitted by Sue and Don Lloyd

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 4 hot or medium Italian sausages
  • 2 cups penné or corkscrew-shaped pasta
  • 1 tsp vegetable oil
  • 1 large onion, chopped
  • 3 gloves garlic, minced
  • 1-28 oz can of tomatoes, coarsely chopped
  • 2 tsp dried basil
  • 1 tsp dried oregano
  • ½ cup fresh parsley, chopped
  • pepper
  • 2 tbsp freshly grated parmesan cheese
  • 2-3 pinches of crushed red pepper

Instructions

In skillet, cook sausage over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes. Drain well. Cut into thin, round slices. Cook pasta until tender but firm. Drain and return to pot.

item.title: Chicken Tetrazzini
item.description:
Submitted by Christine Shank

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 boiling fowl (5 ½ - 6 lbs)
  • a few stalks of celery, chopped
  • 3 small onions, chopped
  • 1 quart milk
  • ¼ lb butter
  • 4 ozs hard flour
  • 2 cups chicken stock
  • 1 lb spaghetti
  • 1 lb mushrooms
  • ½ cup parmesan cheese
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Boil fowl. Take meat from bones and put stalks of celery, onions and salt and pepper to taste into chicken stock. Simmer.

item.title: Angel Hair Pasta with Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Allison and Dave Rudback

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp olive oil, divided
  • 2 boneless chicken breasts, cubed
  • 1 carrot, sliced diagonally
  • 1 cup broccoli florets
  • 2 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 12 oz angel hair pasta
  • 2/3 cup chicken broth
  • 1/4 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1 tsp dried basil

Instructions

Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a medium sized skillet over medium heat. Add chicken and cook until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove from skillet and drain on paper towel.

item.title: Sugar Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp flour

Instructions

Place the above ingredients in a saucepan. Add 1 cup of canned milk. Cook over low heat, stirring until dissolved. Remove from heat. Slowly add 1 beaten egg, stirring constantly. Pour into an 8 inch, unbaked pie shell. Bake at 400 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 300 degrees and continue baking for 30 minutes, or until thick.

item.title: Pizza Dough
item.description:
Submitted by Paulette and Mike Shank

Cousins of the Bride

In a large bowl, combine:

  • 1 ½ cup warm water
  • 1 tbsp yeast

Let sit for 10-15 minutes. Mix well, then add:

Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp oil
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 3 cups flour (+1 cup if needed)
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Mix well and knead to form a ball. Cover and let rise in oven (heat off) for 1 hour.

item.title: Never Fail Pastry
item.description:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 5 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 lb shortening
  • 1 egg, slightly beaten
  • cold water
  • 2 tbsp vinegar

Instructions

Sift flour, baking powder and salt together. Add brown sugar. Cut in shortening until fine as meal. Slightly beat egg in measuring cup. Add cold water until egg and water mixture measures ¾ cup. Add vinegar. Drizzle egg mixture over flour mixture and stir with a fork until it is well mixed.

item.title: Meat Pies
item.description:
Submitted by Del Gosson

Aunt of the Bride

Filling:

  • 3 lbs of beef, or cheaper cut of steak
  • 2 lbs of pork, remove fat
  • 2 double chicken breasts, cooked for about 20 minutes
  • 3 large onions
  • 1 tbsp salt
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • ½ cup flour

Pie Crust:

Ingredients

  • 1 lb of shortening

Instructions

Follow pastry recipe on shortening box. Add 2 extra cups of flour and enough water to hold the pastry together.

item.title: Flapper Pie Special
item.description:
Submitted by Gisele and Ronnie Roy

Cousins of the Bride

  • 18-20 graham wafers
  • ¼ cup butter, melted
  • ¼ cup shortening, melted
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon

Crush and roll wafers fine. Blend in butter, shortening and cinnamon. Line bottom and sides of a 9 inch pie plate. Reserve 1/8 cup for topping.

Filling:

Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 ½ tbsp cornstarch
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Separate eggs. Beat yolks until lemony in color. Add sugar and cornstarch and blend well.

item.title: Deluxe Pumpkin Cream Cheese Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Charleen and Jim Martin

Cousins of the Bride

Requires 2 uncooked pie shells.

  • 1 large pkg cream cheese
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Beat the above ingredients together and spread in bottom of pie shells.

Pumpkin Layer:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups pumpkin, canned
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 cup milk
  • ½ tsp ginger

Instructions

Mix ingredients and spread carefully over cream cheese layer in pie shells.

item.title: Apple Pie
item.description:
Submitted by Yolande and Dave Worthington

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 VERY large Granny Smith apples (6 cups)
  • 2/3 to 1 cup white sugar, depending on tartness of apples
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch

Instructions

Prepare the “Never Fail Pastry” on page 92. Use 2 portions.

Mix sugar, cinnamon, salt and cornstarch together. Peel and thinly slice the apples. Toll out one portion of the pastry and line the bottom of a 9 inch pie pan. Place alternate layers of apples and sugar mixture in the pie pan. Roll out the second portion of pastry, cut vents and place top crust in position. Seal edge and flute.

item.title: Swiss Steak Slow Cooker
item.description:
Submitted by Murdena and Robert Burton

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1-2 steaks
  • 1-14 oz can of tomatoes
  • 1 can mushroom pieces
  • 1 can beef consommé
  • 1 small onion, chopped
  • salt, pepper and garlic salt to taste

Instructions

Brown steak and onion. Season with salts and add remaining ingredients in slow cooker.

Simmer for 2-3 hours.

item.title: Sweet 'n' Sour Meatloaf
item.description:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell

Parents of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 small onion, minced
  • ¼ cup crushed crackers (or bread crumbs)
  • 1-8 oz tin tomato sauce
  • ¼ cup brown sugar, packed
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1 tsp prepared mustard
  • 1 egg
  • 2 lbs ground chuck
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper

Instructions

Mix tomato sauce with sugar, vinegar and mustard until sugar is dissolved. Put ½ cup of sauce mixture aside.

item.title: Sweet and Sour Meatballs
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

  • 2 lbs hamburg
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup bread crumbs
  • 2 eggs

Knead all ingredients together. Form meatballs and place on a cookie sheet. Cook at 350 degrees until brown.

Sauce:

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp dry mustard
  • ¾ cup ketchup
  • ¾ cup water
  • ¾ cup vinegar

Instructions

Mix ingredients together in a sauce pan. Bring to a boil. Mix

item.title: Stuffed Pork Chops
item.description:
Submitted by Kara MacDonald

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • ½ pkg mushrooms, diced
  • 1 red pepper
  • 1 envelope of Lipton onion soup mix
  • 1 cup bread crumbs
  • ½ tsp pepper
  • 4 thick pork chops

Instructions

Combine vegetables and butter in sauce pan and cook until mushrooms are done. Add the soup mix, bread crumbs and pepper. Allow the mixture to cool.

Cut the pork chops on the side and make a pocket on the inside of the pork chop. Do not remove excess fat from the pork chops. Stuff the pockets with the above mixture.

item.title: Roast Shoulder of Pork With Stuffing
item.description:
Submitted by Pauline and Jim Cunningham

Friends of the Groom’s Family (Uncle Jim)

Stuffing:

Ingredients

  • 1/4 – 1/2 cup salt pork, diced
  • 5 apples, diced
  • 1/2 cup celery, chopped
  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 1/2 cup onions, chopped
  • 2 cups dried bread crumbs
  • 1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped

Instructions

Fry salt pork until crisp. Remove from skillet. Cook celery, onion and parsley in fat for 5 minutes. Remove from skillet. Place apples in skillet and sprinkle with sugar. Cook covered until tender. Remove cover and continue to cook until juice evaporates. Add bread crumbs and other ingredients and mix well.

item.title: Roasted Bull Elephant
item.description:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 1 elephant roast
  • ½ boxcar of bread crumbs
  • 3 crates of cut celery
  • 3 mailbags of chopped garlic cloves
  • 2 trash cans of scalding hot water
  • 50 lbs diced hot peppers
  • 1 keg salt
  • 2 oil drums of butter

Instructions

Blast off the rear end of one elephant to make large opening for stuffing. Mix ½ boxcar of bread crumbs, 3 mailbags of chopped garlic, 3 crates of cut celery and 2 trash cans of scalding hot water to moisten.

item.title: Rich's Sweet Balls
item.description:
Submitted by Anya and Rich Pagé

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 pkg of ground beef
  • ½ cup or so of bread crumbs
  • 1 egg
  • salt and pepper
  • 2 green onions, chopped
  • 1 tsp parsley flakes
  • 2 cloves of garlic, chopped
  • butter or oil to grease frying pan
  • 1 jar of honey garlic sauce (available in Chinese food section
  • of grocery store)

Instructions

In a large bowl, mix everything except honey garlic sauce and roll lovingly into balls. The balls like to be squeezed and played with so don’t hesitate to give them your full attention. If you drop a ball on the floor, throw it out, no one likes hairy balls.

item.title: Pork With Broccoli Stir Fry
item.description:
Submitted by Tina Sasyniuk and Kevin Long

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • ¾ lb lean, boneless pork
  • 1 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp cooking sherry
  • 1 bunch broccoli
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 2 tbsp minced garlic cloves
  • 2 tbsp minced gingerroot
  • ¼ cup water
  • 2 tbsp hoisin sauce (optional) 25ml

Instructions

Slice pork thinly across the grain.

In a bowl, stir together cornstarch, soy sauce, and sherry. Add pork and stir to coat well. Separate broccoli into florets, peel stalks, then cut in 1 ½ inch pieces.

item.title: Poor Man's Lobster
item.description:
Submitted by Shelley Bzdell and Marc Robidas — Friends of the Groom

Ingredients

  • Fish filet
  • 7UP (enough to boil in)
  • Garlic butter

Instructions

Boil fish in 7UP until the filet floats.

Fry in garlic butter.

Enjoy the fine taste of lobster!

item.title: No Peek Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Lacey Pigeau

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cups white rice
  • 1-10 oz can condensed celery soup
  • 1-10 oz can condensed mushroom soup
  • 1 pkg dried onion soup
  • 1 cup milk
  • 4-5 skinless chicken breasts

Instructions

Mix soup, milk and rice. Place mixture in a casserole dish. Lay chicken on top. Cover with foil and cook at 325 degrees for 2 hours. Do not peek!

item.title: Manilla Chicken
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie Lemaire and John Breton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup onion, finely chopped
  • 1/3 cup white vinegar
  • 1/3 cup soya sauce
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 2 tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Arrange chicken breasts in a single layer in a large saucepan. Mix remaining 8 ingredients in a small bowl. Pour over chicken. Cover. Bring to a boil. Lower heat and simmer for

item.title: Gord's Homemade Jerky
item.description:
Submitted by Patsy and Gord Bell

Sister-in-law and Brother of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ kg meat (any kind, no fat)
  • 1 bottle soya sauce
  • ½ bottle Worcestershire sauce
  • ½ - 1 cup of brown sugar
  • a few cloves of garlic

Instructions

Cut meat into 1/8-1/4 inch thick strips. Make sure there is no fat on the meat. Cut with the grain for chewier jerky.

Mix the rest of the ingredients and taste to your liking. Add meat strips, cover and refrigerate for at least 24 hours.

item.title: Dan's Chili
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 cup onions, chopped
  • 1 cup celery, chopped
  • 1 can kidney beans
  • 1 tbsp chili powder
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp vinegar
  • 1 cup green peppers
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 large can tomatoes (I use stewed whole tomatoes)
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ½ - 1 tsp crushed red pepper

Instructions

Brown beef, onion, celery, and green peppers. Add remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour stirring frequently.

item.title: Crock Pot Beans
item.description:
Submitted by Tom Alexander

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 3 cups beans
  • 6 cups water
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 onion, chopped
  • ½ lb green bacon or regular bacon (I use a chunk of salt pork)
  • ½ cup molasses
  • 1 cup ketchup
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 2 tsp dry mustard

Instructions

Place everything in the crock pot and let cook on low for 15-20 hours or on high for 5-7 hours. (7 hours seemed to work best)

item.title: Chicken Parmesan
item.description:
Submitted by Charlene and Leonard Gillis

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • ¼ cup parmesan cheese
  • 2 cups tomato sauce (or leftover spaghetti sauce)
  • ½ - 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

Instructions

Put ½ cup of sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place the chicken on top of the sauce and pour remaining sauce over chicken.

Sprinkle parmesan cheese over the sauce and chicken and place in a preheated 350 degree oven. Bake for about 1 ½ hours or until chicken is tender when poked with a fork.

item.title: Chicken à la King
item.description:
Submitted by Rachelle and Tom Pigeau — Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups chopped, cooked chicken breast
  • Frozen vegetables (optional)
  • 1 can cream of chicken soup
  • 1/2 can milk (or water)
  • 1/2 cup mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1 pouch Stove Top stuffing mix
  • 1 cup water

Instructions

Layer chicken and frozen vegetables in a medium baking dish.

Mix cream of chicken soup with 1/2 can of milk (or water) and pour over top of chicken and vegetables.

item.title: Becky's Sweet and Sour Chicken Wings
item.description:
Submitted by Becky and Darren Shackleton

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 large package of wings (about 25)
  • ¾ cup of soya sauce
  • ½ cup ketchup
  • ½ cup water
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 tsp flour

Instructions

Take a large glass casserole dish and mix soya sauce, ketchup, water and brown sugar. Add the flour and mix well. Add the wings in the dish and place in oven for 40 minutes at 400 degrees.

item.title: Bacon Mushroom Swiss Meatloaf
item.description:
Submitted by Darcy Lloyd

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 12 ozs of bacon, diced
  • 4 medium white mushrooms, chopped
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 1 ½ lbs extra lean ground beef
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ cup evaporated milk or half and half
  • 6 ozs shredded swiss cheese
  • ½ cup corn flake crumbs

Instructions

In a large skillet, cook bacon until crisp but not burnt. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel to drain and cool. Remove all but a tablespoon of the bacon fat from the skillet and sauté the mushrooms and onions until tender. Allow to cool slightly.

item.title: Yuck-a-Flux (The Party Drink)
item.description:
Submitted by Kathy and Sean Demarce

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Take one clean industrial size olive or pickle jar (4 litre size would be perfect) with a lid.

Ingredients

  • 3 lemons
  • 3 limes
  • 3 oranges

Instructions

Cut the fruit into quarters. Squeeze the juice into the jar and place the rinds into the jar as well. Add some different fruit like whole strawberries, quartered kiwi (2-3) is good. We recommend NOT using grapefruit, or bananas because they can get mushy. Add 1 bottle of flavoured Vodka (750ml) (Absolut Mandarin, Blueberry, Raspberry, whatever flavour sounds good). Fruit flavoured vodka is the best, although, Vanilla Vodka can be good.

item.title: Porch Climber
item.description:
Submitted by Susy Boulanger and Craig Lavigne

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 big bucket
  • 3 bags of ice
  • 6 cans of concentrated fruit juice
  • 60-100oz of Vodka
  • 24 bottles of beer

Instructions

Pour beer and vodka into bucket. Add ice and concentrated juice. Mix well.

Dunk your cup in and enjoy!

item.title: Jesse's Strawberry Chocolate Shake
item.description:
Submitted by Jesse Beaudry

Nephew of the Bride

Ingredients

  • ½ cup sliced strawberries
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • 1 tbsp cocoa
  • 1 cup milk, divided
  • ½ cup cold club soda
  • ice cream or whipped cream
  • fresh strawberries

Instructions

In a blender, combine the sliced strawberries, sugar, cocoa and ½ cup milk. Cover and blend until smooth. Add remaining ½ cup milk and club soda. Cover and blend. Pour into 2 glasses. Garnish with scoop of ice cream or whipped cream and whole strawberries.

item.title: Yummy Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Francine Massicotte

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1-8 oz pkg of cream cheese

  • 1 cup sour cream

  • ½ cup mayonnaise

  • 1 cup seafood sauce

  • 2 cups shrimp (optional)

  • ½ cup diced green onions

  • ½ cup diced tomatoes

  • ½ cup green peppers

  • 2 cups grated mozzarella and/or cheddar cheese

Instructions

Mix the first 3 ingredients together and spread it on a pizza pan or large plate. Spread seafood sauce on top and add shrimp (optional). Cover with cheese. Sprinkle with green onions, tomatoes and green peppers.

item.title: Vegetable/Chip Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Brenda and Denis Arsenault

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 cup sour cream
  • 1 cup Hellman’s mayonnaise
  • 1 tsp beau monde spice
  • 1 tsp minced onion
  • 1 tsp accent
  • 1 tsp dill weed
  • 1 tsp parsley
  • ½ tsp garlic salt
  • 2 to 3 drops of Tabasco

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together and serve with vegetables or chips.

item.title: Taco Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Diana Bell

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 pkg of cream cheese
  • 1 envelope of taco seasoning
  • 1 jar of salsa
  • 1 medium tomato, diced
  • 1 can black olives, optional
  • 1 small pkg of shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1 small container of sour cream

Instructions

Blend cream cheese and taco seasoning. Spread on baking dish. Layer on sour cream and salsa. Top with diced tomato and black olives. Sprinkle cheddar cheese on top.

item.title: Party Cheese Ball
item.description:
Submitted by Kelly and Kyle Jolley

Friends of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2-250 gr pkgs of Philly cream cheese
  • 2 cups shredded, old cheddar cheese
  • 1 tsbp each of chopped red peppers, green peppers and onions
  • 3 tbsp mayonnaise
  • 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 1 tsp each of seasoning salt, onion salt and garlic salt
  • 1 tsp real lemon juice
  • dash of cayenne pepper
  • pinch of salt

Instructions

Cream cheeses, add remaining ingredients and mix. Chill for one hour. Shape into ball and roll in chopped pecans.

item.title: Nacho Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Kathy Genore and Paul Hamilton

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 package Philadelphia cream cheese
  • 2 tbsp mayonnaise
  • 1 small jar salsa
  • 2 green onions, finely chopped
  • 1 tomato, finely chopped
  • approximately 2 cups finely grated cheddar cheese (marble
  • works well)

Instructions

Cream Philly and mayonnaise together, spread over bottom of dish, not quite to the edges. Pour salsa over cream cheese mixture. Sprinkle chopped green onions and tomato over salsa. Cover with grated cheddar cheese.

item.title: Mexi-Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Margot Bell and Darryl Fleguel

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 500 mL sour cream
  • 1 pkg taco seasoning mix
  • 1 tomato, diced
  • shredded lettuce
  • 1-2 green onions, chopped
  • ½ lb grated cheddar cheese

Instructions

Layer:

Mix sour cream with taco seasoning mix. On top of sour cream mixture, add cheese, lettuce, diced tomato, and onions.

Serve with taco chips. Easyandyummy!

item.title: Hot Crab Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Mary and John Willemsen

Friends of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup milk
  • 1/3 cup salsa
  • 3-8 oz packages of cream cheese, cubed
  • 2-8 oz packages of mock crab, flaked
  • 1 cup thinly sliced green onions
  • 1-4 oz can of chopped green chillies
  • assorted crackers

Instructions

Combine milk and salsa in a bowl. Transfer to a slow cooker coated with non-stick cooking spray. Stir in cream cheese, crab, onions and chillies. Cover and cook on low for 3-4 hours, stirring every 30 minutes. Serve with crackers. Yields 5 cups.

item.title: Guacamole Dip
item.description:
Submitted by Margot Bell and Darryl Fleguel

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 ripe avocados, peeled and mashed
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • 2 tsp onion flakes
  • 1 tsp salt (optional)
  • ¼ tsp cayenne pepper
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • 1 ripe tomato, diced

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. Serve with corn chips, tortilla chips or fresh, raw veggies. Makes about 1 cup.

item.title: Traditional Gingerbread Man Cookies
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie and Shawn Thomas

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 5 to 5 ½ cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp cloves
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 ¼ cups unsulphured molasses
  • 2 eggs, beaten

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Thoroughly mix flour, soda, salt and spices. Melt shortening in large saucepan. Cool slightly. Add sugar, molasses and eggs. Mix well. Add four cups of dry ingredients and mix well. Turn mixture onto lightly floured surface. Knead in remaining dry ingredients by hand. Add a little more flour, if necessary, to make a firm dough. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to ¼ inch thickness for cut-out cookies. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet (small and medium-sized cookies for 6-10 minutes, large cookies for 10-15 minutes). One recipe of this dough will yield 2 to 3 dozen average-sized cookies.

item.title: Refrigerator Balls
item.description:
Submitted by Buddy Pierce

Nephew/Godson of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 can of Eaglebrand
  • 3 cups miniature marshmallows
  • ½ cup maraschino cherries, chopped
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 ½ cups graham cracker crumbs

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. Butter hands and roll into 1 inch balls. Roll in fine coconut.

Refrigerate. Makes about 60.

Uncle Billy’s favorite!!

item.title: Oile Bolen (Dutch Donut)
item.description:
Submitted by Audrey and John Gosson

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • ½ cup corn syrup
  • 4 ½ cups flour
  • 5 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 3 tbsp melted shortening or salad oil
  • ½ cup milk
  • apples chopped in small pieces, raisins and candied fruit-
  • should equal 2 cups total

Instructions

Beat egg yolks until thick. Add sugar gradually and then corn syrup. Mix shortening or oil with milk and stir into the egg and sugar mixture.

item.title: Granny Gosson's Ginger Sparklers
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • ¼ cup molasses
  • ¾ cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 ½ tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large bowl, cream sugar, margarine, egg and molasses. In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients. Blend dry ingredients into cream mixture.

Roll batter into balls and place on a greased cookie sheet. Using a fork, gently press down on balls to flatten slightly. Sprinkle cookies with sugar. Bake at 375 degrees for 8-10 minutes.

item.title: Cookie Candies
item.description:
Submitted by Michelle and Jesse Webster

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 cup soft butter
  • ¾ cup icing sugar
  • 1 tbsp vanilla
  • 2 cups flour & ¾ tsp salt sifted together
  • ½ cup rolled oats
  • ½ cup semi sweet chocolate pieces
  • ½ cup milk
  • sprinkles of your choice

Instructions

Mix butter, sugar and vanilla. Cream well. Add flour mixture (sifted) and rolled oats. With hands, make into balls, roll into logs or use cookie cutters. Cook at 325 degrees for 20 minutes.

item.title: Boiled Raisin Cookies
item.description:
Submitted by Jenny Navid

Aunt of the Bride

  • 2 cups raisins
  • 1 cup water

Boil together for 5 minutes.

Ingredients

  • 4 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp allspice
  • ½ cup chopped nuts
  • 2 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients. Add beaten eggs with the raisins and water. Drop by the tablespoon onto a cookie sheet.

item.title: Tomato Soup Potatoes
item.description:
Submitted by Hazel Pigeau

Grandmother of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 5 or 6 medium potatoes, sliced
  • 1 can Heinz tomato soup

Instructions

Brown hamburg and onions. Place potatoes in a greased casserole dish. Add hamburg and onions. Mix in tomato soup.

Cook at 350 degrees for 1 hour, or until potatoes are tender.

item.title: Pork Chop and Vegetable Casserole
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 4 pork chops
  • 4 medium potatoes, thinly sliced
  • 2 tomatoes, chopped
  • 4 carrots, thinly sliced
  • 1 cup onion, chopped
  • ½ cup green pepper, diced
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 2 chicken bouillon cubes
  • 1 ½ cups boiling water
  • 2 tbsp butter

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Trim fat from chops. Brown chops on both sides, season with salt and pepper to taste.

item.title: Perogy Lazy Boy
item.description:
Submitted by Kim and Raymond Roy

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 10 lasagna noodles
  • 6 cups mashed potatoes (about 12 medium potatoes)
  • 3 cups grated, medium cheddar cheese
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 ¼ cup butter or margarine
  • 2 cups chopped onions
  • bacon bits if desired

Instructions

Cook noodles according to directions on the package.

Line the bottom of a greased 9x13 inch pan with some of the noodles. In large bowl, mix mashed potatoes with cheese and salt. Place a layer of this mixture on top of the noodles. Put second layer of noodles over top and so on.

item.title: Lazy Perogy Casserole
item.description:
Submitted by Joyce Roy

Aunt of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 9 lasagna noodles
  • 2 cups (1-500 gm container) of cottage cheese
  • 1 egg
  • 1/4 tsp onion salt
  • 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese (old)
  • 2 cups mashed potatoes
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 1/2 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup chopped onions and bacon

Instructions

Line a 9x13 inch pan with cooked noodles. Mix cottage cheese, egg and salt together. Spoon over noodles.

item.title: Kapusta
item.description:
Submitted by Rachel and Wayne Pigeau

Godparents/Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 or 4 onions, chopped
  • ¼ cup butter
  • 1 jar of sauerkraut
  • 1 cup of Uncle Ben’s rice
  • 2 cups of water
  • chicken cubes for broth

Instructions

Cook onions in butter until soft. Mix chicken cubes in water. Add rice and cook for 20 minutes until water is absorbed.

In a casserole dish, mix rice, onions and sauerkraut and cook in a slow, 250 degree oven for about 1½ to 2 hours.

item.title: Carrot Casserole
item.description:
Submitted by Judy and Lorne Pierce

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 6 carrots, sliced (boil for about 10 minutes)
  • 1 medium onion
  • 1/4 cup margarine
  • 1/8 cup flour
  • 1 cup milk
  • grated cheese (as much as you like)
  • 1 cup cracker crumbs
  • 3/4 tsp salt
  • 1/4 tsp dry mustard
  • 1/8 tsp celery salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Cook onions in margarine in microwave for 5 minutes. Add flour and spices and mix. Add milk. Cook in milk until thick, about 5-10 minutes in microwave.

item.title: Broccoli Bake
item.description:
Submitted by David Henri

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups white Minute Rice, cooked
  • 1 can of cream of chicken soup
  • ½ cup mayonnaise
  • lemon juice to taste
  • pepper to taste
  • salt to taste
  • ¼ tsp curry powder or more as per your taste
  • 1 head of broccoli florets
  • 2-3 skinless, boneless, cooked chicken breasts

Instructions

Place cut up, cooked chicken pieces on bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place broccoli florets on top of chicken. Mix the rice, soup, mayonnaise, lemon juice and spices together. If rice mixture seems dry, add more mayonnaise. Pour over chicken and broccoli.

item.title: Yogurt Blueberry Muffins
item.description:
Submitted by Ruth Divver

Aunt of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 cup plain yogurt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup bran
  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup blueberries

Instructions

In a small bowl, mix yogurt andoneteaspoon of baking powder. Set aside.

In a large bowl, mix all other ingredients, including the second teaspoon of baking powder. Mix the yogurt mixture into the large bowl.

item.title: Toffee-Topped Bars
item.description:
Submitted by Monique Racine-Theriault

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups firmly packed brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ cup butter, softened
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips
  • ½ cup walnuts
  • ¼ cup flaked unsweetened coconut

Instructions

Mix the brown sugar and flour together. Using a pastry cutter or two knives, cut in the butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Remove one cup of mixture and set aside.

item.title: Texas Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Sherry and Paul Beaulieu

Cousins of the Bride

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 1/2 cups white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-19 oz can of crushed pineapple

Combine all ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch glass pan. Cook at 325 degrees for 45-55 minutes. Insert a toothpick in the centre of the cake to check if it’s ready.

Sauce:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2/3 cup Carnation milk
  • 1 cup coconut
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Combine margarine, brown sugar and milk in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Continue boiling sauce for 5 minutes and then add coconut and vanilla.

item.title: Sucre a creme
item.description:
Submitted by Yvette and Gilles Guenette

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 3 tasses de sucre brun
  • 2 tasses de sucre blanc
  • 5 tasses de crème
  • 4 cuillers à soupe de cornstarch
  • ¼ à ½ tasse de noix hachés

Instructions

Faire chauffer le sucre et la crème jusqu’à ébullition. Retirer du feu et ajouter le cornstarch. Diluer tout en brassant et ajouter les noix. Laisser refroidir avant de déposer dans vos tartes. Ces tartes ont un dessous et un dessus de pâte.

item.title: SINamon Buns
item.description:
Submitted by Louise Dubyk and Don Broughtor

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • 2/3 cup butter or margarine
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • 6-8 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup raisins
  • 1/4 cup cold butter
  • 1 cup cold milk

Instructions

Put flour, sugar, baking powder and salt in a large bowl. Cut in 1/4 cup of butter or margarine. Make a well in the centre. Pour milk into well. Stir to form soft dough (add a bit more milk if necessary).

item.title: Stock Club Cake with Coffee Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Dean Pigeau and Karen Cosgrove

Cousin of the Bride

Cake:

  • 1 1/2 cup flour
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 1/2 tsp soda
  • 1/4 cup cocoa
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 7/8 cup water

Bake for 1 hour or less at 350 degrees.

Coffee Icing:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 tsp coffee
  • 1 tbsp margarine
  • 2 tsp boiling water
  • 1 tsp cocoa
  • 1 cup icing sugar

Instructions

Mix coffee, margarine, water and cocoa until smooth. Slowly add icing sugar until creamy.

item.title: Sour Cream Coffee Cake with Strueusel
item.description:
Submitted by Christine and Graham Baker

Cousins of the Bride

A rich sour cream coffee cake which may be layered or simply topped with tart cranberries and spicy sweet streusel. Wonderful served warm with ice cream or whipped cream.

Streusel:

  • ¾ cup lightly packed brown sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup all purpose flour
  • ¼ cup butter

Mix together sugar, flour, and cinnamon. Blend in butter until crumbly.

Cake:

Ingredients

  • ½ cup soft butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup dairy sour cream
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 cups cranberries, fresh or frozen

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, then vanilla. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture. Add sour cream.

item.title: Skor Bars
item.description:
Submitted by Lisa and Derry Thibeault

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 box of Ritz crackers, finely crushed
  • 4 Skor chocolate bars, finely crushed
  • 1 can of Eaglebrand
  • 1 tub of chocolate icing

Instructions

Put ½ cup of Skor pieces aside for topping.

Combine Ritz crackers and Eaglebrand, mix well. Mix in remaining Skor pieces.

Pour into a greased 8x8 inch pan. Bake at 300 degrees for 20 minutes. Let cool for 20 minutes. Frost with chocolate icing and sprinkle remaining Skor pieces on top.

item.title: Rhubarb Muffins
item.description:
Submitted by Marie and Will Sayer

Cousins of the Bride

  • 3 cups rhubarb, diced
  • 1 1/3 cups whole wheat flour
  • 1 1/3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1 cup yogurt or sour cream
  • 1/2 cup vegetable oil
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Topping:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup chopped nuts
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine rhubarb, flours, brown sugar, baking soda and salt.

item.title: Picnic Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Shirley and Rob Webster

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Cake:

  • ½ cup margarine
  • 2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 ¼ cup white sugar
  • 1 tbsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup peanut butter
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup milk
  • 2 eggs

Cream margarine and sugar. Add peanut butter, vanilla and eggs. Beat well. In a separate bowl, blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to creamed mixture, alternating with milk. Mix well after each addition.

item.title: Pep's Favorite Brownies
item.description:
Submitted by Colette and Mike Peplinskie

Sister and Brother-in-law of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 ½ cup all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • ½ cup cocoa
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup oil
  • 4 eggs
  • ¼ cup cold water
  • ½ cup (or more) chopped walnuts

Instructions

Place all ingredients in a beater bowl in the order given, ending with walnuts. Beat on low speed or by hand until smooth. Do not beat for too long.

item.title: Peppermint Squares
item.description:
Submitted by Chantal and Angus Muir

Cousins of the Groom

  • ½ cup margarine or butter
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • 3 tsp cocoa
  • 1 egg
  • pinch of salt
  • ½ tsp vanilla

Melt above ingredients together until smooth. Add 2 cups of graham wafer crumbs and 1 cup of coconut. Press firmly into a 9x9 inch pan.

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup margarine or butter
  • 2 cups icing sugar
  • 2 tbsp hot milk
  • ½ tsp peppermint extract

Instructions

Mix above ingredients and spread over first layer in pan. Pour a mixture of 3 melted squares of semi-sweet chocolate and 1 tablespoon of margarine or butter over top. Put in the fridge. Cut into squares before it gets too hard. Store in fridge.

item.title: OREO Cookie Dessert
item.description:
Submitted by Fern Henri

Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 square box of vanilla ice cream
  • 1 container of Cool Whip

Instructions

Cut ice cream up into ½ to 1 inch thick pieces to fill the bottom of a 9 inch square pan or a 9x13 inch pan.

Crush OREO cookies until crumbly. Spread on top of ice cream. Pour chocolate syrup over cookies.

Spread Cool Whip over top. Sprinkle more crushed cookies over Cool Whip to decorate.

item.title: Mix-In-The-Pan Carrot Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell

Parents of the Groom

  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 ½ cup shredded carrots
  • ½ cup oil
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • ¼ cup water
  • icing sugar, optional
  • ½ tsp each of salt, baking soda and baking powder

With fork, in an 8 or 9 inch square pan, mix flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.

item.title: Malva Pudding Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Claire and Marc Massicotte

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Cake:

  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tbsp apricot jam
  • 1 cup flour
  • l tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • generous pinch of salt
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp white vinegar
  • 1 cup milk

Beat the egg and sugar well with a mixer. In a separate bowl, combine the jam, sifted flour, soda, baking powder and salt. In another bowl, melt butter and add the vinegar. Add liquids to egg mixture alternately with the flour.

item.title: Ice Box Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Lorna and Zoran Popovitch

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 30 marshmallows, chopped fine
  • 30 graham wafers, rolled fine
  • 18 cherries, chopped
  • ½ cup walnuts, chopped
  • 1 can Eaglebrand Milk
  • 1 cup coconut

Instructions

Mix the first four ingredients with the can of Eaglebrand. Butter a shallow pan and spread thick with half the coconut.

Spread the Eaglebrand mixture over the coconut, then spread another layer of coconut on top. Pack well and put in the fridge for 24 hours.

item.title: Granny Sayer's Rum Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Pat and Tom Sayer

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

  • 1 yellow Duncan Hines cake mix
  • 1 small instant Jello vanilla pudding
  • 4 eggs
  • ½ cup Crisco oil
  • ½ cup water
  • ¼ cup White Rum

Mix above ingredients together and bake in a greased bundt pan to which ¼ cup of chopped pecans has been added. Bake at 350 degrees for 55 minutes.

Topping:

In a sauce pan, combine the following:

item.title: Graham Coconut Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Margaret and Walter Smith

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter, room temperature
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup medium coconut
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • pinch of salt

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar together. Add beaten egg. Add other ingredients and mix well.

Place in a 9x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Serve with ice cream or whipped cream.

item.title: Drumstick Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Kevin Roy

Cousin of the Bride

Base:

  • 1 ½ cups vanilla or graham crumbs
  • ½ cup chopped peanuts or walnuts
  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 2 tbsp peanut butter

Mix the above ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch pan. Save some of the mixture for the topping.

Filling:

Ingredients

  • 250 gr cream cheese
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 4 eggs, add one at a time
  • 1 large container Cool Whip

Instructions

Mix the above ingredients and layer on top of the base.

item.title: Chocolate Eclair Squares
item.description:
Submitted by Amanda McCabe and Jason Simard

Friends of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 container of chocolate icing
  • 1 box of graham crackers
  • 1 pkg of instant vanilla pudding
  • ½ cup of sugar
  • 500 mL whipping cream

Instructions

Prepare vanilla pudding according to the directions on the package. Set aside.

Whip up whipping cream, slowly adding sugar until peaks form. Fold whipping cream into vanilla pudding.

Line an 8x10 inch baking dish with graham crackers. Pour in half of the pudding mixture. Cover pudding mixture with another layer of graham crackers. Pour second half of pudding mixture over graham crackers. Line mixture with graham crackers.

item.title: Chocolate Butterscotch Almond Crunch
item.description:
Submitted by Diane Laderoute

Cousin of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 ¾ cup flour
  • ¾ cup white sugar
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup margarine or butter
  • 2 egg yolks
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • 1 pkg milk chocolate chips
  • 1 cup slivered almonds

Instructions

Combine flour and sugars. Mix margarine in until blended. Add egg yolk and vanilla. Mix until smooth.

Press with floured fingers onto a 10x15 inch cookie sheet with sides. Bake at 350 degrees for 13-18 minutes or until light golden.

item.title: Chipits Chewy Nut Bars
item.description:
Submitted by Diane and Harold Bell

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • ½ cup margarine
  • 2 cups packed brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 ½ cup all purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup semi-sweet Chipits
  • 1 cup coarsely chopped nuts

Instructions

Melt margarine in a large pot. Remove from heat. Add sugar and vanilla stirring until well blended. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to the pot and mix well.

item.title: Cherry Chews
item.description:
Submitted by Anne Mary and Jean-Paul Racine

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Bottom:

  • l cup sifted flour
  • 1 cup rolled oats
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup butter

Mix flour, oats, brown sugar, soda and salt. Add butter and mix until crumbly, first with a fork, then with fingers. Place mixture into bottom of a greased 9x13 inch pan. Bake for 10 minutes.

Filling:

  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp almond extract
  • 2 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 cup coconut
  • ½ cup pecan halves
  • 1 cup well drained maraschino cherries, halved

Beat eggs. Stir in brown sugar, almond flavoring, flour, baking powder, salt, coconut, and maraschino cherries and stir to blend. Pour over first mixture and spread evenly. Sprinkle with pecans. Return to oven and bake 25 minutes until lightly browned. Ice with Cherry Almond icing. Cut into squares or bars to serve. Makes 2 dozen 1½x2 inch bars.

item.title: Cherry Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Stella and Ben Daigle

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • ½ cup melted butter
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1-8 oz pkg of Philadelphia cream cheese
  • 1 large pkg of Dream Whip
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 can cherry pie filling

Instructions

Mix graham crumbs, melted butter and brown sugar together. Press into an 8x12 inch pan.

Mix Dream Whip according to directions on package. Add cream cheese and white sugar to whipped Dream Whip. Spread over graham crumbs. Top with cherry pie filling.

item.title: Caramelized Pear & Apple Bread Pudding
item.description:
Submitted by Judy and Roger Lafreniere

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 2 pears, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges
  • 2 apples, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 5 cups french bread, cut into ½ inch cubes
  • ½ cup raisins
  • ½ cup pecans, coarsely chopped
  • 2 ¼ cups real whipping cream
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large frying pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add fruit and cook , stirring occasionally until tender. Stir in brown sugar, increase heat and cook for 3 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in the bread, raisins and pecans. Set aside.

item.title: Caramel Pecan Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Sandra Stewart

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 ready-to-use Graham pie crust
  • 20 caramel squares
  • 3 tbsp milk
  • ½ cup chopped pecans
  • 2 pkgs cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 eggs

Instructions

Microwave caramels and milk until melted (about 2 min; stir after 1 min). Stir in pecans and mix well. Pour into pie crust and chill for 10 minutes.

Beat (medium speed) softened cream cheese, sugar and eggs until smooth. Pour into chilled pie crust.

item.title: Blueberry Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Joanne and Greg Craig

Cousins of the Bride

Crust:

  • ½ cup butter
  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • ¼ cup sugar

Topping:

  • 2 pkgs Dream Whip
  • 1 pkg cream cheese
  • ½ cup sugar

Filling:

Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • ¼ cup water
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 4 cups fresh/frozen blueberries

Note: Above filling can be substituted for 2 cans of blueberry pie filling.

item.title: Birthday Cake with German Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Louise and Dan Pigeau

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Cake:

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 1/4 cup sugar
  • dash of salt
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 2 eggs
  • 3/4 cup oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Mix first four ingredients together, then add remaining ingredients. Bake at 350 degrees for 30-45 minutes

German Icing:

Ingredients

  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2/3 cup brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup can milk
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1/4 tsp salt

Instructions

Boil until it starts to thicken and add 1 ½ cup of coconut and ½ cup of nuts.

item.title: Banana Cake with Caramel Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Shirley and Dave Jodouin

Cousins of the Groom

Cake:

  • 2 ½ cups flour
  • 1 ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 ½ tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 ½ cup white sugar
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup mashed bananas
  • 1 cup buttermilkor1 cup sourmilk (add 2 tbsp vinegar to your regular cup of milk)

Throw all the ingredients into a big bowl and mix. Pour into a 3 L pan that has been greased, and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes or until a knife inserted in the middle comes out clean and dry.

item.title: Apple Crisp
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 32 Ritz crackers, crushed
  • 1 ½ cups brown sugar
  • ¼ cup flour
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup butter
  • 4 cups chopped apples

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together except for the apples. Place half of the apples on the bottom of a baking dish. Add half of Ritz mixture on top of apples. Place remaining apples and then remaining Ritz mixture on top.

Bake at 375 degrees for 30 minutes.

item.title: Zucchini Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Patti and Ray Racine

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup oil
  • 2 cups grated zucchini
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flour

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Makes 2 loaves.

item.title: Bruschetta and Brew
item.description:
Submitted by Tiffany Breton and Matt Rainville

Friends of the Groom

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup white wine vinegar
  • 2 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp fresh, chopped parsley
  • 1 tsp each of sugar and dried basil
  • ½ tsp dried dillweed
  • ¼ tsp each of salt and pepper
  • dash of hot pepper sauce
  • 1 clove of garlic, minced
  • 2 tomatoes, chopped
  • 1 cucumber, chopped
  • 1 french baguette (or bread)
  • feta cheese for topping

Instructions

In a bowl, combine all ingredients (except cheese), stir, set aside.

item.title: Banana Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 3/4 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/4 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/3 cup shortening
  • 2/3 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • 1 cup mashed bananas

Instructions

Blend flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt together. In another bowl, cream shortening and sugar together. Add beaten eggs and blend. Add dry ingredients alternately with bananas.

Beat after each addition until well blended. Pour into a loaf pan and let stand for 20 minutes. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.  Poke it in the middle and if it’s still doughy-raw, then kick ’er back in for another 5 mins.

item.title: Beer Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Ingredients

  • 3 cups self-rising flour
  • 1 tbsp sugar
  • 1 six-pack of beer
  • 1 tbsp butter, melted

Instructions

Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.

In a large bowl, blend the flour and sugar. Quickly mix in one can of beer. Place the mixture in a 9x5 inch loaf pan. Wet your fingers and smooth the top.

Bake for 1 hour or until golden brown. Drink the other 5 beer while you’re waiting.

item.title: Summer Pizza
item.description:
Submitted by Danielle Beaudry

Niece of the Bride

2 pkgs Pillsbury Dough Croissant Rolls

Flatten on a cookie sheet and bake at 350 degrees for 10-12 minutes, or until golden brown. Let cool.

Topping:

  • ½ cup Miracle Whip
  • ½ cup Hellman’s Mayonnaise
  • 1 pkg Hidden Valley Ranch Dressing (powder)
  • 8 oz cream cheese

Mix together and spread on cooled Croissant crust.

Sprinkle with chopped veggies:

Ingredients

  • 1 cup broccoli
  • 1 cup green pepper
  • ½ cup green onions
  • ½ cup olives
  • 1 cup tomatoes

Instructions

Cover with saran wrap and pat lightly. Best if made the day before.

item.title: Ranch Style Tortilla Roll-Ups
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Darrell Lamothe

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 pkgs Philadelphia cream cheese
  • 1 pkg ranch style dry mix
  • 8 large tortilla shells
  • 1 jar of drained and finely chopped, jalapeno peppers
  • red pimentos, drained and finely chopped
  • onion to your taste, finely chopped
  • black olives, finely chopped, optional

Instructions

Mix cream cheese with dry ranch mix. Spread evenly over tortilla shells. Sparingly top tortillas with the rest of the ingredients.

item.title: Gazpacho
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Joe Albrechtas

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 large tin tomato juice
  • 1 can diced tomatoesor3-4 ripe, fresh tomatoes, chopped
  • ½ English cucumber, unpeeled and finely chopped
  • ½ red pepper, finely chopped (whole one if small)
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced or crushed
  • ½ red onion, finely chopped
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • ¼ cup red wine vinegar
  • approximately 2 tbsp good olive oil
  • dashes of Tabasco to taste
  • salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Best refrigerated for a couple of hours or overnight. Serve cold, garnished with croutons.

item.title: Crab Finger Puffs
item.description:
Submitted by Nancy and Mike Potvin

Cousins of the Groom

  • 8 oz light cream cheese, softened
  • 2 tbsp grated onion
  • 4.2 oz canned crab meat, drained and cartilage removed
  • 2 tbsp fine bread crumbs
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1/16 tsp garlic powder
  • 1 lb. pkg wonton wrappers
  • cooking oil for deep frying

Mash cream cheese in a bowl. Work in onion, crab meat, bread crumbs, salt and garlic powder.

Place 1 tsp of mixture in center of each wonton wrapper. Dampen edges of wonton with water. Fold one corner at a time to centre, pressing to seal well. Cover with damp towel until ready to cook.

item.title: Cache Bay Chicken and Spinach Roll-Ups
item.description:
Submitted by Lorraine and Frank Nault

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 cup spinach, chopped
  • 1/4 cup Dijon mustard
  • 1/2 cup yogurt
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp black pepper
  • 1/4 cup lemon juice
  • 4 large boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 6 slices Swiss Cheese
  • 4 thin slices smoked turkey breast
  • 4 slices lemon (optional)

Instructions

In a bowl combine garlic, spinach, mustard, yogurt, salt, half the pepper and 1 tsp of lemon juice. Gently pound the chicken breasts flat between sheets of plastic wrap. Spread the sauce on the chicken. Top each breast with a slice each of the Swiss cheese and smoked turkey. Cut remaining slices of cheese in half and set aside.
